theorems.name: excluded_zero_implies_no_slot_loss
theorems.claim: excludedSlots = 0 implies both missingSlots and invalidPresentedSlots are zero in the Nat count model

theorems.name: slot_partition_total
theorems.claim: under the slot partition definitions, excludedSlots = 0 forces seenIndicesCount = treeSize and validVotes = treeSize

theorems.name: fully_verified_implies_all_required_success
theorems.claim: the summary model can return fully_verified only when every required check succeeds

theorems.name: fully_verified_implies_no_unknown_checks
theorems.claim: the summary model can return fully_verified only when no unknown checks are present

theorems.name: fully_verified_implies_required_roles_success
theorems.claim: proof verification, tally completeness, and user inclusion roles must all succeed for fully_verified

theorems.name: canonical_vote_order_total
theorems.claim: the abstract input-commitment vote ordering relation is total

theorems.name: canonical_encoding_permutation_invariant
theorems.claim: canonical input-commitment encoding is invariant under permutation of the modeled vote encodings

theorems.name: pack_bits_length
theorems.claim: LSB-first bitmap packing emits exactly ceil(bitLength / 8) bytes

theorems.name: pack_bits_get_bit
theorems.claim: for in-range bits, unpacking the modeled packed byte at i / 8 and i % 8 returns the source bit

theorems.name: accepted_votes_count_tally
theorems.claim: the abstract guest fold keeps the candidate-indexed tally total equal to validVotes

theorems.name: acceptVote_increments_selected_candidate
theorems.claim: accepting a well-formed vote increments the candidate bucket selected by the vote choice

theorems.name: valid_votes_count_accepted
theorems.claim: the abstract guest fold's validVotes count equals the stateful accepted-record count

theorems.name: rejected_records_classification
theorems.claim: rejectedRecords equals the length of the retained rejection-reason classification list

theorems.name: duplicate_index_rejected_before_marking
theorems.claim: a duplicate in-range index is rejected before the presented slot is marked again

theorems.name: invalid_choice_marks_seen_slot
theorems.claim: a fresh in-range record with an invalid choice marks the slot as seen but does not increment validVotes

theorems.name: invalid_inclusion_reserves_commitment
theorems.claim: a record with a valid fresh computed commitment reserves that commitment before inclusion-proof rejection

theorems.name: processVotes_fold_invariant
theorems.claim: the abstract guest fold preserves duplicate-free seen indices, in-range seen indices, and validVotes <= seen-index count

theorems.name: processVotes_seen_indices_length_le_treeSize
theorems.claim: duplicate-free in-range seen indices produced by the guest fold cannot exceed treeSize

theorems.name: zero_exclusion_guest_model_complete
theorems.claim: excludedSlots = 0 over processVotes forces the fold output to have no missing slots and no invalid presented slots

theorems.name: no_overflow_under_guest_bounds
theorems.claim: under explicit guest tree-size, vote-count, and candidate tally-bucket bounds of 1,000,000, modeled seen, valid, rejected, and candidate tally bucket counts remain inside the Rust u32 domain

assumptions: SHA-256 collision resistance is assumed, not proved by Lean
RISC Zero receipt soundness is assumed, not proved by Lean
Rust and TypeScript implementation correspondence is checked by generated vectors and tests
The guest model is an abstract state machine over presented records, not a direct Rust verification

Rust bounded-counter correspondence is claimed only for accepted guest inputs satisfying the explicit Phase 4 guest bounds: treeSize <= 1,000,000, vote count <= 1,000,000, and each candidate tally bucket <= 1,000,000
nonClaims: Lean does not prove SHA-256 collision resistance
Lean does not prove RISC Zero receipt soundness
Lean does not prove AWS runtime behavior
Lean does not prove React rendering behavior
Lean does not prove production-election security
